questions.question: What does http.get<User[]>('/api/users') return?
options: User[] immediately (synchronous)
Promise<User[]>
Observable<User[]> — a lazy stream that starts when subscribed
HttpResponse<User[]> — already containing the data

questions.question: What is the advantage of using the async pipe over manually subscribing in ngOnInit?
options: The async pipe batches multiple HTTP requests into one
The async pipe automatically subscribes and unsubscribes — no memory leak, no ngOnDestroy needed
The async pipe caches the response and avoids duplicate HTTP requests
The async pipe converts the Observable to a Promise for simpler handling

questions.question: HttpParams is immutable. What does const p = params.set('page', '2') do?
options: Modifies params in-place and returns the same object
Creates and returns a NEW HttpParams instance with the 'page' parameter added
Appends '&page=2' to the URL string
Throws an error because HttpParams cannot be modified after creation

questions.question: What HTTP status code does Angular's HttpClient treat as an error (causing the error callback to fire)?
options: Only 500 and above
4xx and 5xx — any non-2xx response
Only network errors (status 0) — HTTP errors are in the response body
401 and 403 only — all others are treated as success

questions.question: How do you set up HttpClient in a standalone Angular application?
options: Import HttpClientModule in the root NgModule
Add HttpClient to the @Component imports array
Call provideHttpClient() in the providers array of app.config.ts
Angular includes HttpClient automatically — no setup needed
